description: Schema fragment (menu_system.json). Merged into src/schema.json by src/schema/build.py. Do not load directly -- cross-fragment $ref uses '#/definitions/...' against the bundled output.

menu_system.description: /system -- top-level system menu (identity, clock, NTP, scripts, scheduler, package, routerboard, health, watchdog, leds, ...).

menu_system_leds.description: /system/leds -- per-LED action rows (via `_items`) plus the `settings` singleton sub-menu controlling global LED behaviour.

settings.description: /system/leds/settings -- singleton row controlling global LED policy (e.g. all-leds-off schedule).

filter.description: Unused for singletons; ignored if present. Example: omit.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.system.clock`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"production clock"`.

time-zone-name.description: IANA time-zone name applied to the router. Example: `Europe/Berlin`.

time-zone-autodetect.description: If true, RouterOS picks the zone via GeoIP/cloud and ignores `time-zone-name`. Example: `false`.

dst-active.description: Read-only indicator that DST is currently in effect; typically not set in IaC. Example: omit.

date.description: Wall-clock date. Rarely set by IaC -- prefer NTP. Example: `2026-05-17`.

time.description: Wall-clock time. Rarely set by IaC -- prefer NTP. Example: `14:00:00`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.system.identity`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"core router"`.

name.description: Hostname shown in winbox and CLI prompt. Example: `gw-core-01`.

id.description: Stable IaC identity token rendered into `comment=`. Example: `iac.system.leds`.

id_pad.description: Trailing dot-segments of `id` to retain for ordering. Example: `1`.

comment.description: Free-form text appended after the `id` token in the RouterOS comment. Example: `"dim LEDs at night"`.

all-leds-off.description: When to power off every status LED. Example: `after-1min`.
